rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FLORENCE TRAUTMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the proposed order contained different amounts from those in the executor's supplement, and supplements or amendments to a petition should stand on their own and not refer to "requests" from an Examiner. The California Rules of Court, not the Examiners, require detailed schedules to support statutory fees based on any amount other than the I&A. [CRC 7.705(b).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROGER DWAYNE KRAUSS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Amended Order Appointing Administrator And Amended Letters Of Administration (Probate Code 870)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Neither the petition caption nor the notice of hearing specified "authority to access the decedent's digital assets" as relief sought. CRC 7.50 & 102. 2) The petition requests the Court's finding that the administrator is the decedent's "agent" within the meaning of the Electronic Communications Privacy Act. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUNNE FUNGE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order That Estate Plan Does Not Exist And That Assets Held In Trust Belong To Decedents Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The petition requests the Court's determination that the decedent revoked the trust identified in the grant deed purporting to transfer title to the subject real property to the trust by destroying the governing trust instrument. The petition alleges that the statutory presumption of revocation under Probate Code section 6124 applies to the decedent's trust. The petition requests the Court's determination that the entire subject real property titled in the name of the trust created by the decedent and her predeceased spouse belongs to the decedent's estate, as a result of the presumed revocation of the trust. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES COWDEN BRADFORD III
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/23/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies. No proposed order. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7, and 14.59 E.] Notwithstanding the lack of courtesy copies, the petition was skimmed online as a ONE-TIME courtesy. A non-resident personal representative will be required to post bond. A declaration setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate must be filed for the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] Are all of the persons listed in item 8 ALL of the beneficiaries of the devisee trust (not just the nominated fiduciaries)? The original will file has not been provided to the Examiner yet as of 3/28/22. Counsel is responsible for filing papers, either by e-filing or conventionally, as the case may be; unfiled papers should NOT be sent to an Examiner either for filing or for review. [LRSF 14.7 C.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/12/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/18/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
